vocabularies.definition: This is the time in the morning or evening when it is 7:00.
vocabularies.example:
School starts at seven o’clock.
I wake up at seven o’clock.
Dinner is at seven o’clock.
quizzes: School starts at __________ __________ in the morning.
I wake up at __________ __________ every day.
It is exactly __________ __________ now.

vocabularies.definition: This is the time that is 30 minutes after 12:00.
vocabularies.example:
We have lunch at half past twelve.
It is now half past twelve.
The bus leaves at half past twelve.
quizzes: It is thirty minutes after noon, so it's __________ __________ __________.

vocabularies.definition: This is the time at night when it is 12:00.
vocabularies.example:
The party ended at midnight.
Cinderella left at midnight.
It is dark at midnight.
quizzes: The day ends and a new one starts at __________.
It is very dark outside because it is __________.
Cinderella had to leave the party at __________.

vocabularies.definition: This is the time in the middle of the day when it is 12:00.
vocabularies.example:
The sun is high at midday.
We eat lunch at midday.
It is hot at midday.
quizzes: The sun is high in the sky at __________.
Twelve o'clock in the daytime is called __________.
We usually eat our lunch at __________.

vocabularies.definition: To move your body to music.
vocabularies.example:
They dance to the music.
Can you dance?
We like to dance at parties.
quizzes: The children like to __________ to the music.
Can you __________ like a ballet performer?
We had a party and everyone started to __________.

vocabularies.definition: To speak very loudly, often when you are angry or excited.
vocabularies.example:
Don't shout in the library.
He shouted my name.
Please do not shout.
quizzes: Don't __________ in the library; be quiet.
He had to __________ because the music was very loud.
The teacher told us not to __________ at each other.

vocabularies.definition: To make a sound with your voice when you think something is funny.
vocabularies.example:
The clowns make us laugh.
She laughed at the joke.
It is good to laugh.
quizzes: The funny clown made all the children __________.
We always __________ when we watch a funny movie.
It's good to __________ with your friends.

vocabularies.definition: To use your legs to push your body up into the air.
vocabularies.example:
Can you jump high?
The cat jumped onto the wall.
I jump over the puddle.
quizzes: How high can you __________ in the air?
The frog will __________ into the water.
Don't __________ on the bed, you might fall!

vocabularies.definition: To jump on one leg.
vocabularies.example:
I could hop on one leg when I was four.
Rabbits hop in the field.
Can you hop on your left foot?
quizzes: Can you __________ on one leg like a rabbit?
The little bird started to __________ across the grass.
I saw a kangaroo __________ very fast.

vocabularies.definition: To move with little jumps or to jump over a rope.
vocabularies.example:
She likes to skip in the playground.
I can skip with a rope.
We skip to school.
quizzes: Girls often like to __________ with a rope.
We can __________ to school when we are happy.
She is very good at using a rope to __________.

vocabularies.definition: To wear special clothes, like for a party or a play.
vocabularies.example:
We dress up for the party.
I like to dress up as a pirate.
They dress up in funny clothes.
quizzes: I like to __________ __________ as a superhero.
We always __________ __________ for the Halloween party.
The actors __________ __________ in beautiful costumes.

vocabularies.definition: To go up something using your hands and feet, like a tree or a wall.
vocabularies.example:
Monkeys climb trees very well.
Don't climb that wall.
He climbed the ladder.
quizzes: Be careful when you __________ that tall tree.
Monkeys like to __________ using their hands and feet.
He is trying to __________ the ladder to the roof.

vocabularies.definition: To take and hold something that is moving through the air.
vocabularies.example:
Try to catch the ball.
He caught the train.
Can you catch me?
quizzes: Try to __________ the ball with both hands.
The cat tried to __________ the bird.
Did you __________ the bus this morning?

grammars.description: Ôn tập các từ để hỏi: how, what, when, where, which, who, why.
grammars.example:
Which country are we in?
Why are you running?
What time do they have lunch?

grammars.description: Diễn tả khả năng làm gì đó trong quá khứ.
grammars.example:
I could hop on one leg when I was four.
She could read when she was five.
They couldn't swim when they were young.

vocabularies.definition: A hot drink that you make with leaves and water.
vocabularies.example:
Grandma drinks a cup of tea.
Do you like tea?
I want some tea.
quizzes: My grandmother drinks a hot cup of __________.
Do you want some sugar in your __________?
In England, many people like to drink __________.

vocabularies.definition: A hot, dark drink that many people have in the morning.
vocabularies.example:
Dad likes hot coffee.
This coffee is strong.
She drinks coffee with milk.
quizzes: Adults often drink __________ to stay awake.
My dad likes his __________ with some milk.
The smell of fresh __________ is very nice.

vocabularies.definition: A cold drink made with milk and ice cream.
vocabularies.example:
I want a strawberry milkshake.
This chocolate milkshake is good.
Can I have a banana milkshake?
quizzes: I would like a cold strawberry __________.
You make a __________ with milk and ice cream.
Chocolate __________ is my favorite drink.

vocabularies.definition: A liquid that you put on food to give it more taste, like tomato or chocolate.
vocabularies.example:
Pass me the tomato sauce.
I like chocolate sauce on ice cream.
The sauce is spicy.
quizzes: I like to put tomato __________ on my chips.
This pasta __________ is very delicious.
Be careful, the hot __________ is a bit spicy.

vocabularies.definition: Food made from grain that we usually eat for breakfast with milk.
vocabularies.example:
I have cereal for breakfast.
Do you put milk in your cereal?
This box of cereal is new.
quizzes: I eat a bowl of __________ for breakfast every day.
Do you like your __________ with cold or hot milk?
There's a picture of a tiger on the __________ box.

vocabularies.definition: Long, thin pieces of food made from flour, like spaghetti.
vocabularies.example:
We ate a bowl of noodles.
Chicken noodles are yummy.
She cooks noodles for lunch.
quizzes: I like to eat chicken __________ with a pair of chopsticks.
Would you like some __________ for lunch today?
My mom cooks delicious __________ with vegetables.

vocabularies.definition: A flat, round cake often eaten for breakfast with lemon or sugar.
vocabularies.example:
She made a delicious pancake.
I like lemon on my pancake.
We had pancakes for breakfast.
quizzes: We had a big __________ with honey for breakfast.
Can you flip a __________ in the air?
I like to eat my __________ with sugar and lemon.

vocabularies.definition: Small, red, sweet fruits with seeds on the outside.
vocabularies.example:
These strawberries are sweet.
I like strawberries and cream.
Red strawberries are in the garden.
quizzes: These red __________ are very sweet and juicy.
I picked some __________ from the farm.
Would you like some __________ and cream?

vocabularies.definition: A white, creamy food made from milk.
vocabularies.example:
Yoghurt is good for you.
I eat strawberry yoghurt.
Do you want some yoghurt?
quizzes: I like eating strawberry __________ for a snack.
Is there any __________ in the fridge?
Plain __________ is very good with honey.

vocabularies.definition: Something you use to drink water or juice, made of a clear material.
vocabularies.example:
A glass of milk.
Please give me a glass of water.
The glass is full.
quizzes: Can I have a __________ of cold water, please?
Be careful not to break the _________.
I pour some orange juice into my __________.

vocabularies.definition: Something with a handle that you use for drinking tea or coffee.
vocabularies.example:
A cup of tea.
I broke the cup.
Would you like a cup of coffee?
quizzes: Would you like a __________ of tea or coffee?
The __________ has a handle so it's easy to hold.
Put the __________ on the table carefully.

vocabularies.definition: A container for liquid, like water or milk, made of plastic or glass.
vocabularies.example:
A bottle of water.
The baby has a bottle.
Open the bottle please.
quizzes: Don't forget to take a __________ of water to school.
Is this __________ made of plastic or glass?
Open the __________ and pour the milk.

vocabularies.definition: A round container used for holding soup, rice, or cereal.
vocabularies.example:
A bowl of soup.
Put the fruit in the bowl.
I ate a bowl of rice.
quizzes: I eat my cereal from a large __________.
Put some fruit in the __________ for everyone.
There is some hot soup in the __________.

vocabularies.meaning: đã lái xe (quá khứ của drive)
vocabularies.definition: The past of 'drive' (to move a car or bus).
vocabularies.example:
Yesterday we drove to town.
My dad drove the car.
She drove to the beach.
quizzes: Last Sunday, my dad __________ us to the beach.
We __________ past the mountains on our holiday.
Mom __________ the car to the supermarket yesterday.

vocabularies.meaning: đã nhìn thấy (quá khứ của see)
vocabularies.definition: The past of 'see' (to look at something with your eyes).
vocabularies.example:
We saw the place where May grew up.
I saw a bird in the tree.
He saw his friend yesterday.
quizzes: I __________ a beautiful rainbow after the rain.
We __________ a funny movie at the cinema last night.
They __________ some wild animals in the jungle.

vocabularies.definition: The past of 'teach' (to help people learn something).
vocabularies.example:
The teacher taught us a song.
My dad taught me to swim.
She taught English last year.
quizzes: My teacher __________ us how to speak English.
Dad __________ me how to ride a bike when I was six.
She __________ a new song to the children.

grammars.description: Mệnh đề quan hệ xác định sử dụng who, which, where để làm rõ đối tượng.
grammars.example:
Can you see the woman who is cooking in the kitchen?
This is the town where I lived.
They’re the mountains which I climbed with my father.

grammars.description: Sử dụng thì quá khứ đơn kết hợp với 'when' để kể chuỗi sự việc.
grammars.example:
When we finished lunch we went to the funfair.
When the bus arrived we all got on it.
We were sad when the bus came to take us home.

vocabularies.definition: The part of your body between your neck and your arm.
vocabularies.example:
Put your hand on your shoulder.
My shoulder hurts.
She carried the bag on her shoulder.
quizzes: He carried his heavy bag on his __________.
Touch your __________ and then your head.
The parrot sat on the pirate's __________.

vocabularies.definition: The part of your body that connects your head to your shoulders.
vocabularies.example:
Giraffes have long necks.
My neck is cold.
She wears a scarf around her neck.
quizzes: A giraffe has a very long __________.
I wear a warm scarf around my __________.
He turned his __________ to look at the bird.

vocabularies.definition: The part of your body where your food goes after you eat.
vocabularies.example:
My stomach is full.
He has a big stomach.
The food goes into your stomach.
quizzes: My __________ feels full after the big dinner.
If you eat too much, you might have a __________-ache.
The food goes into your __________ after you swallow.

vocabularies.definition: One of the five long parts at the end of your hand.
vocabularies.example:
He pointed his finger at the map.
I have ten fingers.
She cut her finger.
quizzes: I have five __________s on each hand.
He pointed his __________ at the picture.
She has a gold ring on her __________.

vocabularies.definition: The middle part of your leg that bends.
vocabularies.example:
I hurt my knee when I fell.
Bend your knees.
The baby is on my knee.
quizzes: I hurt my __________ when I fell off my bike.
Bend your __________s when you jump.
The baby sat on her mother's __________.

vocabularies.definition: One of the five small parts at the end of your foot.
vocabularies.example:
Wiggle your toes.
I stubbed my toe.
He has big toes.
quizzes: We have five __________s on each foot.
I can wiggle my __________s inside my shoes.
Be careful not to stub your __________ on the table.

vocabularies.definition: The part in the middle of your arm where it bends.
vocabularies.example:
Don't put your elbows on the table.
My elbow is itchy.
He hurt his elbow playing tennis.
quizzes: The middle part of your arm is called the __________.
Don't put your __________s on the table while eating.
Move your __________ to bend your arm.

vocabularies.definition: The part of your body that is behind you, from your neck to your legs.
vocabularies.example:
He carried the bag on his back.
My back hurts.
Lie on your back.
quizzes: Lie on your __________ and look at the clouds.
He carried his little sister on his __________.
My __________ hurts because I was sitting all day.

vocabularies.definition: A long piece of white cloth that you put around a hurt part of your body.
vocabularies.example:
The nurse put a bandage on his arm.
I need a bandage for my cut.
He has a white bandage.
quizzes: The nurse put a __________ on my hurt leg.
I need a __________ for this cut on my arm.
Keep the __________ clean and dry.

vocabularies.definition: To push air out of your throat with a loud sound when you are sick.
vocabularies.example:
He has a loud cough.
She has a bad cough.
Cover your mouth when you cough.
quizzes: He has a bad __________ because he is sick.
Please cover your mouth when you __________.
The loud __________ woke up the baby.

vocabularies.definition: How hot or cold something is. Doctors check this when you are sick.
vocabularies.example:
She has a high temperature.
The doctor checked my temperature.
I have a temperature today.
quizzes: The doctor used a thermometer to check my __________.
She has a high __________ and needs to rest.
Is your __________ normal today?

vocabularies.definition: When the inside of your neck hurts, often when you speak or eat.
vocabularies.example:
Ice cream is good for a sore throat.
My sore throat hurts when I talk.
He has a cold and a sore throat.
quizzes: It hurts to swallow because I have a __________ __________.
Hot tea with honey is good for a __________ __________.
I can't sing today because I have a __________ __________.

vocabularies.definition: When your back hurts.
vocabularies.example:
Grandpa has backache.
My dad got backache yesterday.
Yoga is good for backache.
quizzes: Grandfather has __________ after working in the garden.
Carrying a heavy bag can give you a __________.
I need to rest because I have a bad __________.

vocabularies.definition: When your stomach hurts.
vocabularies.example:
Don't eat too much or you'll get a stomach-ache.
I have a stomach-ache.
She stayed home because of a stomach-ache.
quizzes: I have a __________ because I ate too many green apples.
The little girl cried because she had a __________.
If you have a __________, you should drink some water.

grammars.structure: Review of comparative and superlative adjectives
grammars.description: Ôn tập so sánh hơn và so sánh nhất của tính từ.
grammars.example:
Jenny is taller than you.
Ivan is the strongest man here.
This book is more interesting than that one.

I’m not as strong as you.
The Nile isn't as big as the Amazon River.
Is it as high as Everest?

grammars.description: Cấu trúc muốn hoặc cần làm gì.
grammars.example:
I want to go to the party.
You need to drink water.
She wants to buy a new dress.

vocabularies.definition: The large, round object that shines in the sky at night.
vocabularies.example:
The moon is bright tonight.
There is a full moon.
The moon goes round the earth.
quizzes: The __________ is very bright in the sky tonight.
Neil Armstrong was the first man to walk on the __________.
The __________ goes around the Earth every month.

vocabularies.definition: The space above the earth where you can see clouds, the sun, and stars.
vocabularies.example:
There are no clouds in the sky.
The sky is blue.
Birds fly in the sky.
quizzes: The __________ is blue and there are no clouds.
Look up at the __________; can you see the airplane?
At night, the __________ is dark and full of stars.

vocabularies.definition: A small, bright light that you see in the sky at night.
vocabularies.example:
Look at that shining star.
There are millions of stars.
The star is very far away.
quizzes: The little __________ is shining brightly in the night sky.
A __________ is a giant ball of burning gas very far away.
Can you see the North __________ from your window?

vocabularies.definition: A place where water falls from a high place down to a lower place.
vocabularies.example:
We swam near the waterfall.
The waterfall is loud.
Water falls down the waterfall.
quizzes: The water is falling down the mountain into a __________.
We heard the loud sound of the __________ from far away.
The __________ looks beautiful with the rainbow in it.

vocabularies.definition: A hot, wet place with many trees and wild animals.
vocabularies.example:
Many animals live in the jungle.
The jungle is green.
We walked in the jungle.
quizzes: Tigers and monkeys live in the hot, wet __________.
It is very difficult to walk through the thick __________.
The __________ is full of tall trees and colorful birds.

vocabularies.definition: A living thing that grows in the ground and needs water and sunlight.
vocabularies.example:
Water the plant every day.
This plant has red flowers.
Don't step on the plant.
quizzes: Please remember to water the __________ in the garden.
A green __________ needs sunlight and water to grow.
The little __________ is starting to grow a new leaf.

vocabularies.definition: A line of water that moves across the top of the sea.
vocabularies.example:
The surfer rode the big wave.
The waves are high today.
We jumped over the waves.
quizzes: The surfer successfully rode the giant __________.
We love jumping over the __________s at the beach.
The sea has many white __________s today because it's windy.

vocabularies.definition: The earth and all the people, animals, and places on it.
vocabularies.example:
It's the highest mountain in the world.
We live in a big world.
She wants to travel the world.
quizzes: Mount Everest is the highest mountain in the __________.
We live on a beautiful planet in a giant __________.
One day, I want to travel around the __________.

vocabularies.definition: A piece of land that has water all around it.
vocabularies.example:
They sailed to a desert island.
We live on an island.
The island has nice beaches.
quizzes: They were shipwrecked on a small desert __________.
An __________ is a piece of land with water all around it.
We spent our summer holiday on a tropical __________.

grammars.description: Trạng từ chỉ cách thức (well, badly, loudly, quietly, quickly, slowly...).
grammars.example:
Please speak quietly.
He runs quickly.
She sings beautifully.

grammars.description: So sánh hơn đối với trạng từ.
grammars.example:
Bears can run more quickly than monkeys.
Monkeys can climb better than tigers.
Elephants can move faster than snakes.

vocabularies.definition: A yellow metal that is very expensive and used for jewelry.
vocabularies.example:
The king wears a gold crown.
She has a gold ring.
The medal is gold.
quizzes: The champion won a beautiful __________ medal.
That ring is very expensive because it is made of __________.
The king wears a __________ crown on his head.

vocabularies.definition: A shiny, grey metal that is used for jewelry and coins.
vocabularies.example:
She has silver wings.
The car is silver.
I have a silver coin.
quizzes: The coins in my pocket are made of _________.
Second place in the race gets a __________ medal.
She wore a beautiful __________ necklace to the party.

vocabularies.definition: Something that has a lot of light or is a very strong color.
vocabularies.example:
He wears a bright orange shirt.
The light is very bright.
She likes bright colours.
quizzes: The sun is very __________ today, so wear your sunglasses.
She wore a __________ yellow dress to the summer party.
The stars are very __________ in the clear night sky.

vocabularies.definition: A color that is pale and not dark, or something that does not weigh a lot.
vocabularies.example:
Light blue is my favorite color.
This bag is very light.
I need a light jacket.
quizzes: I prefer __________ green to dark green for my bedroom.
The feathers of a bird are very __________.
Can you switch on the __________? It's too dark here.

vocabularies.definition: A color that is close to black, or a place where there is no light.
vocabularies.example:
It's getting dark outside.
He has dark hair.
The room is dark.
quizzes: It gets __________ very early during the winter months.
She has long, __________ hair and brown eyes.
I am afraid of the __________ when I am alone at home.

vocabularies.definition: A pattern with long lines of different colors.
vocabularies.example:
Zebras are striped animals.
He wore a striped tie.
The flag is striped.
quizzes: Zebras are famous for their black and white __________ fur.
He wore a blue and red __________ shirt to the game.
The cat has a long, __________ tail.

vocabularies.definition: A pattern with many small round dots of different colors.
vocabularies.example:
The clown has spotted trousers.
I saw a spotted dog.
Her dress is spotted.
quizzes: The ladybird is red with small black __________ on its back.
She chose a __________ dress for the birthday party.
His dog has a white coat with large brown __________.

vocabularies.definition: The part of a bird, insect, or plane that it uses for flying.
vocabularies.example:
Birds have wings to fly.
The fairy has wings.
The plane has big wings.
quizzes: The bird hurt its __________ and couldn't fly away.
An airplane has two large __________s to help it stay in the air.
The butterfly has beautiful colorful __________s.

vocabularies.definition: A thick, stiff kind of paper that we use for making boxes or masks.
vocabularies.example:
This box is made of card.
We use card to make masks.
Draw on the card.
quizzes: We used some old __________ boxes to make a toy castle.
You should draw your picture on a piece of thick __________.
Is this birthday __________ made of paper or card?

vocabularies.definition: A hard, strong material like iron or gold that we use to make tools or cars.
vocabularies.example:
The scissors are made of metal.
Gold is a metal.
The gate is metal.
quizzes: Most cars are made of strong __________ like steel.
Gold and silver are very precious __________s.
The gate at the park is made of heavy black __________.

vocabularies.definition: A hard material that you can see through, used for windows and bottles.
vocabularies.example:
Be careful, it's made of glass.
The window is glass.
Drink from a glass.
quizzes: Be careful with that vase; it is made of __________ and might break.
We can see through the windows because they are made of __________.
Most bottles for drinks are made of plastic or __________.

vocabularies.definition: A light material that is easy to shape into things like toys or bottles.
vocabularies.example:
This bottle is made of plastic.
Toys are often plastic.
Don't burn plastic.
quizzes: Many of my toys are made of colorful __________.
Don't forget to recycle your empty __________ bottles.
A __________ bag is not good for the environment.

vocabularies.definition: The soft hair of a sheep that is used to make warm clothes like sweaters.
vocabularies.example:
My scarf is made of wool.
Sheep give us wool.
This jumper is wool.
quizzes: This warm sweater is made of soft sheep's __________.
In the winter, I wear a hat and scarf made of __________.
She bought some blue __________ to knit a new blanket.

vocabularies.definition: The hard material that trees are made of, used to make tables and doors.
vocabularies.example:
The table is made of wood.
We burn wood for fire.
The door is wood.
quizzes: The kitchen table and chairs are made of solid __________.
We need to collect some __________ to start a campfire.
The front door of our house is made of heavy __________.

grammars.description: Hỏi và trả lời về chất liệu của vật.
grammars.example:
What's it made of?
It's made of gold.
They're made of paper.

grammars.structure: shall, could and let's for suggestions
grammars.description: Sử dụng Shall, Could, Let's để đưa ra lời đề nghị.
grammars.example:
Shall we design some props?
We could use blue paper.
Let's design the sea.

vocabularies.definition: The school subject where you learn about maps and different countries.
vocabularies.example:
We learn about maps in geography.
I like geography class.
Geography is interesting.
quizzes: In __________ class, we learned how to read different maps.
We are studying the mountains and rivers of Asia in __________.
Do you know the capital city of France? We learned it in __________.

vocabularies.definition: The school subject where you learn about things that happened in the past.
vocabularies.example:
History tells us about the past.
We study Romans in history.
Do you like history?
quizzes: I love learning about the ancient kings and queens in __________.
__________ class helps us understand what happened in the past.
We went to a museum to learn more about the __________ of our city.

vocabularies.definition: Games like football or tennis that you play for exercise.
vocabularies.example:
I play sports on Tuesdays.
Football is a sport.
We do sports outside.
quizzes: What is your favorite __________ to play at school?
Playing __________ is a great way to stay healthy and fit.
My brother is very good at many different __________, like tennis and soccer.

vocabularies.definition: The school subject where you learn about numbers and shapes.
vocabularies.example:
Maths is about numbers.
I am good at maths.
We have a maths test.
quizzes: We learned how to add and subtract large numbers in __________.
I need a ruler and a compass for my __________ homework.
Is __________ your favorite subject at school?

vocabularies.definition: The school subject where you learn about nature and how things work.
vocabularies.example:
We do experiments in science.
Science explains the world.
I love science class.
quizzes: Today in __________ class, we did an experiment with water and oil.
I want to be a scientist and study __________ when I grow up.
We learned about the different parts of a flower in __________.

vocabularies.definition: Information Technology - the school subject where you use computers.
vocabularies.example:
We use computers in IT.
IT stands for Information Technology.
